style_prompt: She enthusiastically delivers her message with a normal speaking speed.

content: Then was Sir Galahad glad, and unarmed him, and said he would abide there three days.
style_prompt: With enthusiasm, she conveys her message with a normal tone.

content: And when Sir Melias heard him speak: Sir, he said, for God's love let me not die in this forest, but bear me unto the abbey here beside, that I may be confessed and have my rights.

content: Sir Galahad answered: Sir knight, come on your peril.
style_prompt: Speaking at a normal pace, her tone maintains a clear high-pitched quality.

content: And then he turned again unto Sir Melias, and there he alighted and dressed him softly on his horse to fore him, for the truncheon of his spear was in his body; and Sir Galahad stert up behind him, and held him in his arms, and so brought him to the abbey, and there unarmed him and brought him to his chamber.
style_prompt: Speaking energetically, she maintains a fast pace and heightened volume.

content: So rode he away from them and met with a squire that said: Knight, those knights in the castle defy you, and defenden you ye go no further till that they wit what ye would.

content: And therewithal the other smote him on his shield great strokes, so that their spears brake.

content: And when the devil saw your pride and presumption, for to take you in the quest of the Sangreal, that made you to be overthrown, for it may not be enchieved but by virtuous living. Also, the writing on the cross was a signification of heavenly deeds, and of knightly deeds in God's works, and no knightly deeds in worldly works.
style_prompt: A woman with a loud sound and a usual tone.

content: So it happened on a day the duke's daughter said: Ye have done unto me great wrong to slay mine own father, and my brother, and thus to hold our lands: not for then, she said, ye shall not hold this castle for many years, for by one knight ye shall be overcome.
style_prompt: Her regular tone enhances the energy in her words.

content: Sir Melias beheld this adventure, and thought it marvellous, but he had no hunger, but of the crown of gold he took much keep; and therewith he stooped down and took it up, and rode his way with it.

content: Then Sir Galahad drew out his sword, and set upon them so hard that it was marvel to see it, and so through great force he made them to forsake the field; and Galahad chased them till they entered into the castle, and so passed through the castle at another gate.
style_prompt: A woman with a loud sound and her usual tone.

content: Then Sir Melias blessed him and said: Fair lord of heaven, help and save thy new made knight.

content: Now, said Galahad, is she here for whom this castle was lost? Nay sir, said the priest, she was dead within these three nights after that she was thus enforced; and sithen have they kept her younger sister, which endureth great pains with mo other ladies.

content: Then Sir Galahad opened the gates, and saw so much people in the streets that he might not number them, and all said: Sir, ye be welcome, for long have we abiden here our deliverance.

content: Then Galahad drew out his sword and smote off the left arm of him, so that it fell to the earth.

content: CHAPTER fifteen.

content: And where thou tookest the crown of gold thou sinnest in covetise and in theft: all this were no knightly deeds.

content: Then looked Sir Galahad on his arms that nothing failed him, and then he put his shield afore him; and anon there met him seven fair maidens, the which said unto him: Sir knight, ye ride here in a great folly, for ye have the water to pass over.

content: And then he came into a fair meadow, and there was a fair lodge of boughs.

content: And then by great force they held all the knights of this castle against their will under their obeissance, and in great service and truage, robbing and pilling the poor common people of all that they had.

content: And therefore is it called the Maidens' Castle, for they have devoured many maidens.
